layman_headline: The crypto market is scared and most altcoins look beaten down — but not beaten down enough to jump in yet.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a 'Fear' state with a score of 28 out of 100. Bitcoin is holding relatively steady around $64,500 but is sucking capital away from alternative coins (56.5% dominance). Most of the swing trade candidates are either already overbought after big rallies (KAITO, DEXE) or stuck in bearish downtrends without a clear reversal signal. There's geopolitical risk this weekend around oil markets that could create volatility.
layman_action: Stay patient and keep your cash ready. The market hasn't given a clear low-risk entry signal yet. If Ethereum dips toward $1,800 with rising volume and RSI drops below 35, that could be the moment to act. Don't chase anything that's already rallied 40%+.
expert_reasoning: Scanning all candidates: KAITO and DEXE are overbought (RSI 79 and 71 respectively) after 44% and 63% 5-day rallies — chasing these in a Fear environment with high BTC dominance is a recipe for getting caught in a correction. TRUMP has the most oversold RSI at 22 (strong signal per our credibility data), but the chart bias is -0.70 with no support structure and -97.8% from ATH — this is a knife-catch. BONK dropped 26% in 7 days but has zero technical data to confirm a bottom. ETH is the best risk-adjusted candidate with RSI approaching oversold, massive liquidity, and $41B in ecosystem TVL, but price is still below all moving averages and the chart bias is bearish at -0.70. The proper play is to wait for ETH to either test $1,800 support (better entry) or break above $1,900 resistance with volume confirmation. Weekend geopolitical risk (oil/Middle East per CryptoSlate) adds additional reason to stay on the sidelines.
risk_factors: Geopolitical weekend risk around oil markets could trigger a broad risk-off selloff affecting all crypto
BTC dominance at 56.5% and rising means altcoins may continue underperforming even if BTC holds steady
Fear & Greed at 28 trending flat — no sign of capitulation that would mark a reliable bottom for mean-reversion
watch_list: ETH price action around $1,800 support — a bounce with volume spike would trigger a BUY
Fear & Greed index — a drop below 20 would signal potential capitulation and better entry opportunities
BTC weekend price action given geopolitical oil risks — a break below $63,000 would delay all alt entries
market_context: Market is in Fear (28/100) with BTC dominance elevated at 56.5%, creating a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades. Weekend geopolitical risks around oil markets add uncertainty, suggesting patience over action.
context_banner: Fear-driven market with high BTC dominance and weekend geopolitical risk — the best trade right now is no trade until clearer setups emerge.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 approaching oversold but not yet at the 35 threshold that historically triggers reliable bounces per our 80% win rate data on RSI signals.
Fear & Greed at 28 with flat trend — market is fearful but not yet in extreme fear/capitulation territory that creates the best contrarian entries.
Bitcoin trading through a dangerous weekend with 20% of world's oil supply at risk — geopolitical catalyst for potential volatility in either direction.
ETH derivatives are resetting, suggesting leveraged positions have been flushed — could set up cleaner price action for a directional move.
FTX distributing $900M to creditors in fifth payment round — potential buy-side pressure as recovered funds flow back into crypto markets over coming weeks.
